text:
Once upon an autumn starslit evening, sleepy squirrelurovision. Grayson hadn't slept much because of those noisy birds nesting outside his hole. But tonight would be different.

He wouldn't just listen to them.

With sharp claws, Grayson grabbed the biggest ripe plum anyone had ever heard a squirrel steal. Gathering sticks beside the hollow, he stuffed it with treasures he’d saved during the season: shiny seeds, shiny beetles, and a shiny beetle-patterned feather.

As he chowed down, feathers sliding down his belly, something unusual fluttered through the darkness. Not just any owl...

It was Ollie the Owl. Owls didn’t usually hunt silves.

“Good night!” Ollie sighed, weary from nocturnal vigils.

Grayson couldn’t resist talking.

“Just a snack owl like you?”

Owling gently around the berry. “You don’t have feathers.”

A grin.

“Then keep dreaming.”

And sleepy as ever, Grayson drifted.


There
